title: Podman build multistage does not use local image
body:
### Issue Description

I expect the default policy is "missing". So, if imageA depends on imageB and I'm trying to build them all locally before push podman forces to download imageB (older one) and ignore locally built.

It might be similar to bug https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23055

However, I noticed that this is only happens in multistage build

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I've have Containerfile.test:
```
ARG VERSION=latest
FROM registry.acme.local/docker/bento/ui:${VERSION} as ui
FROM registry.acme.local/docker/bento/base:${VERSION}
```
Both `ui` and `base` have been built locally.


### Describe the results you received

Images from registry were downloaded

### Describe the results you expected

Local images were used

### podman info output

```yaml

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Investigation of the logs I saw suspicious out of nowhere statement
```

comments.body:
@jsirex Could you please try with latest podman ? I tried an I am unable to reproduce this. I built both ` registry.acme.local/docker/bento/ui:latest` and ` registry.acme.local/docker/bento/base:latest` locally first.

```Dockerfile
ARG VERSION=latest
FROM registry.acme.local/docker/bento/ui:${VERSION} as ui
RUN echo hey > hello

FROM registry.acme.local/docker/bento/base:${VERSION}
COPY --from=ui hello .
RUN echo hello
```

```console
[1/2] STEP 1/2: FROM registry.acme.local/docker/bento/ui:latest AS ui
[1/2] STEP 2/2: RUN echo hey > hello
--> 98ff6d82e1cf
[2/2] STEP 1/3: FROM registry.acme.local/docker/bento/base:latest
[2/2] STEP 2/3: COPY --from=ui hello .
--> 9c9516b7f14d
[2/2] STEP 3/3: RUN echo hello
hello
[2/2] COMMIT test
--> 0e034441a97a
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
0e034441a97a13aca12e765d21da46d5f7e645a33089ab21cfe2daa14c1c0842
```

I am closing this issue, please re-open or comment if needed.

```
❯ podman --version
podman version 5.7.0

❯ podman info
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.42.1
...
```

and issue still here. I can't re-open issue b/o permissions.

REPRODUCING:
1. put the repro.sh script into an empty directory
2. adjust registry to some really existing registry you have read/write access
3. run script - it fails

repro.sh:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

# We need write access to this registry to reproduce
REGISTRY=myregistry.corp.local/bug

echo "Generate containers for multistage"
for name in one two three; do
    cat <<EOF > "Containerfile.$name"
FROM debian:trixie-20251117
EOF
    podman build -f "Containerfile.$name" -t "$REGISTRY/fake-image-$name:latest" .
    podman push "$REGISTRY/fake-image-$name:latest"
done

echo "List images"
podman image ls "$REGISTRY/fake-image*"

echo "Change containers locally: add file generation"
for name in one two three; do
    cat <<EOF >> "Containerfile.$name"
RUN echo "name $name" > /$name
EOF
    echo "build, but not push"
    podman build -f "Containerfile.$name" -t "$REGISTRY/fake-image-$name:latest" .
done

echo "List images"
podman image ls "$REGISTRY/fake-image*"

echo "Generate bug container. Expected FROM uses locally generated images"
cat <<EOF > Containerfile.bug
ARG VERSION=latest
FROM $REGISTRY/fake-image-one:\${VERSION} as one
ARG VERSION=latest
FROM $REGISTRY/fake-image-two:\${VERSION} as two
ARG VERSION=latest
FROM $REGISTRY/fake-image-three:\${VERSION} as three

FROM debian:trixie-20251117
COPY --from=one /one /
COPY --from=two /two /
COPY --from=three /three /
RUN env|sort && cat /one /two
EOF

echo "Expect not to download anything"
podman build -f Containerfile.bug .

echo "List images finally"
podman image ls "$REGISTRY/fake-image*"
```

Notice: we generated images and pushed them (id 53f0b37b86c4)
Then changed locally and rebuilt (another uniq image id)

Tried to build container, all ids reverted back to 53f0b37b86c4 (images we pulled, local changes discarded).
Please reopen, I experienced the same issue
